Ukraine Conflict News





30 September 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Secretary of War Pete Hegseth Addresses General and Flag Officers at Quantico, Virginia DoD 30 Sep 2025
  • Unfit, Undertrained Troops No Longer Tolerated DoD News 30 Sep 2025 -- Physical fitness and combat training standards will be raised, said Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, who spoke today to an audience of hundreds of generals, admirals and senior enlisted personnel at Marine Corps Base Quantico, Virginia.
  • Hegseth Says Promotions, Retention to be Based on Meritocracy, Not Quotas DoD News 30 Sep 2025 -- The War Department will focus on lethality and merit, not on political correctness and diversity, said Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, who spoke today to an audience of hundreds of generals, admirals and senior enlisted personnel at Marine Corps Base Quantico, Virginia.
  • Trump: Military Will Protect Nation With Focus on Merit, Reawakened Warrior Spirit DoD News 30 Sep 2025 -- The U.S. military, said President Donald J. Trump, is strong again, with a focus on merit and a warrior spirit.
  • Hegseth Announces War Department Reforms in Sweeping Speech to Top Military Brass DoD News 30 Sep 2025 -- Secretary of War Pete Hegseth today announced a series of new directives aimed at strengthening the War Department's people and culture during an in-person speech in Quantico, Virginia, to the U.S. military's most senior leaders.

  • Smith, Houlahan Condemn Trump Administration's Waste of Time, Resources, and Money on Culture War Pep Rally House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 30 Sep 2025 -- U.S. Representatives Adam Smith (D-Wash.), Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee, and Chrissy Houlahan (D-Penn.), Ranking Member of the Military Personnel Subcommittee, released the following joint statement in response to Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth's gathering of top U.S. military leaders to Quantico.

  • Coast Guard orders additional waterways commerce cutters US Coast Guard 30 Sep 2025 -- The Coast Guard is moving forward with the acquisition of the new Waterways Commerce Cutter (WCC) class, designed to replace the legacy fleet of inland tenders and facilitate commerce vital to the nation's economic security and strategic mobility.
  • Coast Guard awards contract for new heavy weather surf boats US Coast Guard 30 Sep 2025 -- The Coast Guard awarded an indefinite delivery, indefinite quantity (IDIQ) contract on Sept. 29 to Rozema Boat Works, Inc. of Mount Vernon, Washington to acquire up to six second-generation special-purpose craft - heavy weather (SPC-HWX II) boats.
  • USS Benfold (DDG 65) departs U.S. 7th Fleet after 10 years of forward-deployed service US Navy 30 Sep 2025 -- The Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Benfold (DDG 65) departed Commander, Fleet Activities Yokosuka, Japan, Sept. 29, following 10 years of forward-deployed service to U.S. 7th Fleet.
  • Blue Ridge visits Busan, South Korea US Navy 30 Sep 2025 -- U.S. 7th Fleet flagship USS Blue Ridge (LCC 19) arrived in Busan, South Korea for a port visit, Sept. 25, 2025, after completing the trilateral exercise Freedom Edge followed by a visit to Pyeongtaek and Republic of Korea 2nd Fleet. This arrival in Busan marks the first time since 2020 that Blue Ridge has visited Busan.

  • Six Countries Have Already Contributed Over $2 Billion Through the PURL Initiative, and Six More Are Ready to Join President of Ukraine 30 Sep 2025

  • $133 million sustainment contract to bolster ADF air operations Australia DOD 30 Sep 2025 -- The Australian Government is investing up to $133.6 million over five years in command and control capabilities to support Australian Defence Force air operations.
  • Australia - M142 High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems DSCA 30 Sep 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Australia of M142 High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems and related equipment for an estimated cost of $705 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.

  • A successful training launch of newly inducted indigenously developed Fatah-4, Ground Launched Cruise Missile was conducted today by Pakistan Army at a range of 750 Kilometers. ISPR Pakistan 30 Sep 2025 -- A successful training launch of newly inducted indigenously developed Fatah-4, Ground Launched Cruise Missile was conducted today by Pakistan Army at a range of 750 Kilometers.

  • Finland to contribute to safeguarding Denmark's airspace Finnish MoD 30 Sep 2025 -- On the proposal of the Government, the President of the Republic has decided that the Finnish Defence Forces will contribute to safeguarding Denmark's airspace.
  • Norway supports Denmark with assistance in handling drones Government of Norway 30 Sep 2025 -- The Norwegian Government has decided to provide Danish authorities with assistance in dealing with possible drone threats during the high-level meetings taking place in Denmark this week.
  • After Drone Sightings, Copenhagen To Host EU Leaders For 'Acute' Security And Defense Summits RFE/RL 30 Sep 2025 -- In response to recent sightings of drones that disrupted air traffic in Copenhagen and in the Danish city of Aalborg, Denmark is bringing together European leaders in the capital on October 1-2 for two summits on security and defense.

  • Romania, guarantor of European commitment: the second Romanian general officer has taken over command of EUTM RCA Romania - Ministry of National Defence 30 Sep 2025 -- On Monday, September 29th, Brigadier General Claudiu Mădălin Tamaș took over the command of the European Union Training Mission in the Central African Republic (EUTM RCA), from Brigadier General Nicolae-Gabriel Oros, during a ceremony held in Bangui.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 30 September 2025 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 30 Sep 2025

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • PM Netanyahu's Statement with President Donald Trump in the White House Israel - Prime Minister 30 Sep 2025
  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks at the Start of the Government Meeting Israel - Prime Minister 30 Sep 2025

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the announcement of a plan for Gaza UN Secretary-General 30 Sep 2025
  • Gaza: Guterres calls for 'all parties' to commit to US peace plan, as UN agencies stress urgent ceasefire need UN News 30 Sep 2025 -- The UN Secretary-General António Guterres has welcomed Monday's Gaza peace deal proposal from US President Donald Trump, urging "all parties" to commit to it.
  • Breathless in Gaza: Health crisis deepens as families burn plastic for fuel UN News 30 Sep 2025 -- Doctors in Gaza are warning of a surge in respiratory illnesses as families - cut off from basic supplies - burn plastic and cardboard to cook and keep warm. They say the outbreak will worsen unless life-saving medicines, fuel and food are allowed into the devastated territory.

  • Statement Prime Minister of Australia 30 Sep 2025 -- Australia welcomes President Trump's plan to bring peace to Gaza after almost two years of conflict and a devastating loss of civilian life.
  • Doorstop - Abu Dhabi Prime Minister of Australia 30 Sep 2025
  • Poland's position on Gaza peace plan Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Sep 2025

    • Other Conflicts

  • Afghanistan Goes Dark As Taliban Imposes Nationwide Communications Blackout RFE/RL 30 Sep 2025 -- The Taliban has imposed a nationwide shutdown of telecommunications in Afghanistan, a move that has largely cut off the country from the rest of the world.
  • Taliban internet blackout leaves Afghans in limbo while needs surge UN News 30 Sep 2025 -- The United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan (UNAMA) called on Taliban leaders to immediately and fully restore Internet and telecommunications services.

  • Rohingya plight in Myanmar, a 'test for humanity' UN News 30 Sep 2025 -- At the heart of the emergency are the Rohingya Muslims, denied Burmese citizenship, driven from their homes and forced into camps or exile.
  • Secretary-General's remarks at the UN High-Level Conference on the Situation of Human Rights of Rohingya and Other Minorities in Myanmar [as delivered] UN Secretary-General 30 Sep 2025

  • Peace in Democratic Republic of Congo Mostly a Promise, Mission Head Tells Security Council, Stressing That Reality Still Marred with Violence UN Security Council 30 Sep 2025 -- A few months after the signing of a United States-brokered peace accord between Kinshasa and Kigali, the Security Council today met for an open briefing on the situation in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with speakers emphasizing the need for positive steps towards a ceasefire and lasting peace, instead of continuing atrocities and a worsening humanitarian crisis.
  • DR Congo: Peace still elusive despite 'progress we see on paper', UN official says UN News 30 Sep 2025 -- "There are discrepancies between the progress we see on paper and the reality we observe on the ground which continues to be marred with violence," said Bintou Keita, presenting the Secretary-General's latest report.
  • Democratic Republic of Congo: UN expert warns of abuses against women human rights defenders and their families in South Kivu OHCHR 30 Sep 2025 -- A UN expert today expressed grave concern about the abduction and alleged sexual violence perpetrated against four women human rights defenders in South Kivu province, as well as the killing of the mother of one of the activists.

News Reports

  • Prime Minister's Office Announcement Israel - Prime Minister 30 Sep 2025 -- The Government unanimously approved the appointment of Maj.-Gen. David Zini as Director of the ISA for a five-year term. Maj.-Gen. Zini will assume office on 5 October 2025.

  • Russia: Statement by the spokesperson on the withdrawal from the European Convention for the Prevention of Torture and Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment European External Action Service (EEAS) 30 Sep 2025 -- On 29 September, Vladimir Putin signed the law on Russia's withdrawal from the European Convention for the Prevention of Torture and Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment. The formal decision is one more step in Russia's complete disengagement from its international commitments and clearly demonstrates Russia's disregard for the protection of human rights.
  • Second Ukrainian Suspect Detained Over Nord Stream Blasts RFE/RL 30 Sep 2025 -- Polish authorities have detained a Ukrainian man suspected of involvement in the 2022 explosions that damaged the Nord Stream gas pipelines running under the Baltic Sea from Russia to Germany, prosecutors in Warsaw said on September 30.
  • Brussels Eyes Frozen Russian Assets To Finance Ukraine Beyond 2025 RFE/RL 30 Sep 2025 -- With the United States indicating that it may not be willing to finance Ukraine's defense needs for much longer, the European Union is looking into various ways of footing a larger part of the bill in 2026 and 2027, assuming - as most people in Brussels do - that the war will continue.

  • Remarks at the Reception Marking the 76th Anniversary of the Founding of the People's Republic of China PRC MofA 30 Sep 2025
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on September 30, 2025 PRC MofA 30 Sep 2025

  • Iran Confirms Deportation Of 120 Citizens From US This Week RFE/RL 30 Sep 2025 -- Iran says some 120 of its citizens are being deported from the United States and will return home this week.
  • UK applies sanctions on links to Iran's nuclear programme UK FCDO 30 Sep 2025 -- UK applies sanctions on over 70 Iranian individuals and organisations linked to Iran's nuclear programme in response to years of Iranian nuclear escalation.

  • Wider Europe Briefing: Previewing Czech Election And Reviewing Moldova's Vote RFE/RL 30 Sep 2025

  • Armenia is ready to follow the path of peace and democracy Council of Europe 30 Sep 2025 -- Addressing the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe today, Prime Minister of Armenia, Nikol Pashinyan, stressed the preparedness of the people of Armenia to safeguard democracy, fight human rights violations and nurture the "newborn peace" with the help of the Council of Europe and its institutions.

  • Trial begins in killing of former Cambodian lawmaker Radio Free Asia 30 Sep 2025 -- A trial began Tuesday for a man alleged to have shot and killed Cambodian opposition politician Lim Kimya during a brazen daytime attack in the Thai capital earlier this year.
